column.name: title
column.value: The teenager quietly mapping every tree in her city

column.name: summary
column.value:
Most weekends, sixteen-year-old Amara walks a different postcode with a phone in one hand and a tape measure in the other. Two years in, she has logged more than eleven thousand trees — species, height, health — into an open map anyone can use.
It started as a school project and became something the council now quietly relies on. When a developer applies to fell a row of limes, residents cite her data; when a heatwave hits, planners can see exactly which streets have no shade at all.
She is careful to say she is not an expert, just stubborn. But her map has already changed three planning decisions, and a dozen other teenagers in other cities have asked her how to start their own.

column.name: support_question
column.value: Should councils be required to publish an open tree map?

column.name: case_for
column.value: Public data lets residents challenge felling and target shade where it's needed.

column.name: case_against
column.value: Mapping every tree is costly upkeep councils can't reliably fund.

column.name: title
column.value: One minute inside the vertical farm feeding a tower block

column.name: summary
column.value:
On the ground floor of a 1970s tower, a disused storage room now grows salad. Stacked trays climb from floor to ceiling under pink light, fed by water that recirculates and uses a fraction of what a field would drink.
The residents' co-op that runs it says the harvest already covers the building's communal kitchen and a small stall out front. No lorries, no packaging, no supermarket markup — just leaves cut the morning they are eaten.
Critics point out the electricity bill for all that light, and question whether it could ever scale beyond a novelty. The growers counter that the point was never scale — it was showing a block of flats it could feed itself.

column.name: support_question
column.value: Should councils fund vertical farms in social housing?

column.name: case_for
column.value: Fresh food on-site with no lorries, packaging or supermarket markup.

column.name: case_against
column.value: The lighting bill is huge and it may never scale past a novelty.
